← all tutorials

Two-Phase Commit (2PC) — interactive 📖 TWO_PHASE_COMMIT.md 📄 two_phase_commit.py

📖 Full guide  ·  📄 Ground-truth source — every panel below recomputes the same model in JS.
A distributed transaction across 3 shards needs a coordinator to run a strict two-phase handshake: PREPARE (vote) then COMMIT/ABORT (decision). Pick a scenario and scrub the timeline to watch every message. Crash the coordinator and watch the shards go in-doubt (BLOCKED). The five scenarios are recomputed in JS from the identical engine as two_phase_commit.py.  |  Paxos (consensus) →   db 2PC (WAL view) →

① Message sequence — coordinator ↔ participants, step by step

What to do: choose a scenario, then drag the slider (or press play) to reveal messages one at a time. Each lane is a node; arrows are messages; ✕ = crash, ◆ = the global decision point, ↻ = restart/recovery.
YES / COMMIT NO / ABORT / crash decision point restart / recovery / poll (waiting) note / ack

② Scenario timeline — phases, the global decision point, and the crash

What this shows: the whole scenario as one horizontal strip — one cell per message, colored by kind. Phase boundaries and the crash are marked. Click any cell to jump the diagram above to that step. In the coordinator crash scenario, watch the orange poll cells pile up while shards sit in-doubt.

③ Gold check — atomicity: every completed run ends in ONE unanimous decision

What this proves: the page re-runs the 2PC engine in your browser on the same five scenarios as two_phase_commit.py GOLD CHECK and re-derives each terminal-state array. Atomicity means all shards reach the same final state. The blocked scenario is the honest exception — a liveness failure (stuck PREPARED), not an atomicity violation (they still agree). The green badge confirms JS matches the .py exactly.
check: … JS 2PC recompute matches two_phase_commit.py GOLD CHECK (5 scenarios, all unanimous).
